executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware surge with 103 organizations compromised in a single day on April 16, 2026. High-profile victims include Canada Goose (hit by both CoinbaseCartel and ShinyHunters), Markham Stouffville Hospital, National Money Mart (database leaked by Everest), and Peterborough Public Health. The Qilin ransomware gang alone claimed 21 Canadian victims spanning critical sectors including education (Eastern Townships School Board), healthcare (BioPharma Services), and public safety (Workers Health & Safety Centre). This coordinated wave suggests either a mass-exploitation event or systematic targeting of Canadian infrastructure.

items.name: Qilin
items.targets: 21 Canadian organizations including Eastern Townships School Board, Workers Health & Safety Centre, BioPharma Services, NovAtel (Hexagon), Kuzco Lighting, Waterloo Information Systems, and multiple manufacturing/construction firms
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service) - double extortion ransomware with data leak site

items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service)

canada_landscape.summary: Canada experienced a catastrophic ransomware event on April 16, 2026, with 103 organizations compromised across all provinces and critical sectors. Notable victims include iconic retailer Canada Goose (double-compromised), Markham Stouffville Hospital, National Money Mart with confirmed database leak, and municipal government (North Perth). No CCCS advisories were issued in the reporting period despite the severity of the incident wave.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose compromised by two separate threat actors (CoinbaseCartel and ShinyHunters), Active Green + Ross hit by Sinobi
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company - database leaked by Everest ransomware gang; Morison Insurance Brokers compromised by Sinobi

report_2w.executive_summary: Fortinet FortiClientEMS is under active exploitation via CVE-2026-35616, prompting an official alert from the Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS). A critical vulnerability in the widely-used Axios JavaScript library (CVE-2026-40175) enables remote code execution and cloud credential theft, with proof-of-concept code now publicly available. The ShinyHunters threat group has breached both Rockstar Games via compromised Slack credentials and Hallmark, exposing 1.7 million customer accounts. The FBI dismantled a $20 million phishing operation (W3LL), while OpenAI revoked its macOS app certificate following a supply chain compromise in the Axios dependency.

items.description: Vulnerability in Fortinet FortiClientEMS enabling unauthorized access
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: Medium

items.description: CRLF injection vulnerability in Axios JavaScript library enabling remote code execution and cloud credential theft
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: Low

items.name: ShinyHunters
items.targets: Rockstar Games, Hallmark (1.7M accounts), K Subsea Group
items.ttps: T1199 (Trusted Relationship - compromised Slack), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1591 (Gather Victim Org Information)

items.name: W3LL (Phishing-as-a-Service)
items.targets: $20 million phishing operation targeting multiple organizations
items.ttps: T1566 (Phishing), T1078 (Valid Accounts), T1539 (Steal Web Session Cookie)

items.name: CyberAv3ngers (Iran-linked)
items.targets: Water utilities and industrial controllers (SCADA systems, Unitronics PLCs) in United States
items.ttps: T1584 (Compromise Infrastructure), T1133 (External Remote Services), T1491 (Defacement), T1110 (Brute Force)

items.name: Everest (Ransomware)
items.targets: K Subsea Group
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567.002 (Exfiltration to Cloud Storage), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery)

items.name: CozyDuke / MiniDionis (APT29)
items.targets: Multiple organizations via updated CozyCar/MiniDionis malware and SeaDuke variants
items.ttps: T1566 (Phishing), T1204 (User Execution), T1071 (Application Layer Protocol), T1027 (Obfuscated Files or Information)

items.name: ClickFix (Malware Campaign)
items.targets: macOS users via Script Editor and Terminal-based social engineering
items.ttps: T1204.002 (User Execution: Malicious File), T1059.004 (Command and Scripting Interpreter: Unix Shell), T1566 (Phishing)

canada_landscape.summary: The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued Alert AL26-007 for actively exploited CVE-2026-35616 affecting Fortinet FortiClientEMS, marking this as a priority patching target for Canadian organizations. Operation Atlantic, a Canadian-involved law enforcement action, seized $12 million in cryptocurrency linked to phishing operations. No direct breaches of major Canadian retailers or financial institutions were identified in this reporting period.
